items.text:
Mister Quigley,
The doorstead has closed
sooner than expected,
so I'm on my own for the time being.

I trust all is correct on your end,
and you'll reopen when you can manage.
Meanwhile, I'm going to look around.

I'll leave a trail of beacons,
should you send someone after,

and do my best to leave useful information
in case of my disappearance or expiry.

I'm standing in ... an anteroom.
The decor is very strange. The stylings
don't look like any culture I know.

So this may indeed be
a completely other world.

Even if I'm wrong, we've certainly managed
to fling me much further than is possible

with a mundane doorstead. So --
rest assured that I consider my life's work a success,
regardless of what happens to me going forward.

items.comment: (Becoming more outward-focused again with his attention, switching gears).
items.text:
These chambers were made for a large gathering,
but they're empty.
Still, I feel as though I'm being watched.

The surfaces are spotless, no dust.
Is it actively being cleaned?

I'll press forward.
You'll hear from me again,
God willing.
